behavior_summary: U8P_GIRLSSTU::look runs when the player 'looks at' or examines this item and produces a short one-word description (a bark) describing the item's current visual variant.

The routine queries the item's current frame via Item::getFrame(this) and selects one of three descriptive barks inferred from frame ranges: 'perfume', 'brush', or 'mirror' (these words appear repeatedly in the bark nodes).
After emitting the chosen bark the process suspends/ends the U8P_GIRLSSTU::look interaction; the handler does not change global flags, present menus, or modify persistent state.
notable_control_flow: U8P_GIRLSSTU::look performs a numeric frame check and branches into three groups of barks based on the frame value.
Inferred frame-to-description mapping (from observed comparisons): frames 0x00-0x02 -> 'perfume'; 0x03-0x05 -> 'brush'; 0x06-0x07 -> 'mirror'.
Each branch issues a short Bark output and then calls suspend to end the U8P_GIRLSSTU::look process.
open_questions: Are there valid frame values outside 0x00-0x07 and if so what should the U8P_GIRLSSTU::look handler show for those frames? (behavior for out-of-range frames is not present in the extracted nodes.)
Why the bark lines repeat identical text nodes multiple times — is this an artifact of the disassembly or deliberate duplicates for localization/variation?

main_function.name: U8P_GIRLSSTU::look
main_function.purpose: Provide a short, immediate descriptive bark when the player examines the item by mapping the item's current visual frame to a single-word description and then suspending the U8P_GIRLSSTU::look interaction.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the engine when the player examines/looks at the object (process type 0x0208).
Behavior is conditional on the item's current frame (obtained by calling Item::getFrame(this)).

function_map.name: U8P_GIRLSSTU::look
function_map.role: Look/examine handler that chooses and emits a short descriptive bark based on the item's frame

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: Query the current frame/variant of the item (called by U8P_GIRLSSTU::look)

external_calls.purpose: Obtain the numeric frame/index of the item to decide which descriptive bark to play
End/suspend the U8P_GIRLSSTU::look process after delivering the bark

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_GIRLSSTU::look runs when the player 'looks at' or examines this item and produces a short one-word description (a bark) describing the item's current visual variant.
